rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical hydrogenation and reductive coupling of alkenes and alkynes catalyzed by a B12 model complex (heptamethyl cobyrinate) in the presence of TFA
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Co(II)/Co(III) B12 model complex to Co(I), which is protonated by TFA to form a Co(III)-H species that delivers a hydrogen radical to the alkene/alkyne substrate

key_intermediates.name: Co(I) species of B12 model complex (heptamethyl cobyrinate)
Co(III)-H hydride intermediate with H-radical character
benzylic carbanion (for R = electron-withdrawing group such as CO2Et or Br)
carbene from alpha-bromo benzylic carbanion after Br- elimination

electron_flow: Cathode reduces the Co(II/III) B12 model to Co(I), which is protonated by TFA to give a Co(III)-H with H• character; H• transfers to the beta-position of the alkene/alkyne to generate an alpha-benzylic radical that then dimerizes, disproportionates, abstracts H•, or (for electron-poor substrates) is further reduced to a carbanion that is protonated.

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline-N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: ESR detection of DMPO-H adduct (g=2.009, AN=14.9 G, AH=19.7 G) and DMPO-C carbon-centered radical adduct (g=2.008, AN=15.0 G, AH=21.4 G) during electrolysis of styrene

probe_reagents.name: TFA-d1 (deuterated trifluoroacetic acid)
probe_reagents.observation: DMPO-H ESR signal disappeared when TFA-d1 was used in place of TFA, confirming the trapped H originates from the acid via the Co-H intermediate

probe_reagents.name: control without acid
probe_reagents.observation: No reduced product formed in the absence of TFA for all alkene/alkyne substrates, confirming Co-H requires protonation

notes: For styrene derivatives with electron-withdrawing alpha-substituents (R = CO2Et, Br), a second cathodic reduction of the benzylic radical to a carbanion is invoked; with R = Br, elimination of Br- gives a transient carbene that couples with another benzylic carbanion to deliver E/Z 2,3-diphenylbutene. Three competing pathways for ethylbenzene formation from the benzylic radical are proposed: disproportionation, H• abstraction, and reaction with a second Co-H. Phenylacetylene is proposed to be reduced first to styrene, then further to 2,3-diphenylbutanes (racemic/meso) and ethylbenzene at -1.2 V vs Ag/AgCl.
